steps.title: Use one table row in the given model
steps.content: The problem already tells you the linear relationship has the form $f(x)=mx-28$. Use a table row, such as $x=10$ and $f(x)=82$.

steps.title: Solve for the coefficient
steps.content: Substitute the row into the equation: $$82=10m-28.$$ Add $28$ to both sides: $$110=10m.$$ Divide by $10$: $$m=11.$$ The correct free-response answer is 11.
